briefdescription.para: Buffer size for small MQTT topics, payloads, and JSON documents.
Buffer size for large MQTT payloads and JSON documents (recovery data)
Buffer size for reading individual CSV lines.
Timeout for recovery operations in milliseconds (60 seconds)

memberdef.argsstring: (MqttClient &client, const char *topicPrefix, const char *sensorType, const char *sensorId)
memberdef.name: ensureAckInit

memberdef.argsstring: (char *buffer, size_t bufferSize, const char *topicPrefix, const char *sensorType, const char *sensorId, const char *suffix)
memberdef.name: createFullTopic

memberdef.argsstring: (MqttClient &mqttClient, const char *topicPrefix, const char *sensorType, const char *sensorId, float celsius, const DateTime &now, int sequence)
memberdef.name: sendToMqtt

briefdescription.para: Publishes real-time sensor data to the MQTT broker with QoS 1 delivery.

detaileddescription.para: This function builds a JSON payload from the provided sensor data and publishes it to the specified MQTT topic. After publishing, it waits briefly for a PUBACK handshake from the broker to confirm delivery. If no acknowledgment is received within the timeout window, the data is saved to a CSV file for later recovery.

parameterdescription.para: Reference to the MQTT client instance

simplesect.para: true if published and acknowledged by broker, false if fallback to CSV
Uses QoS 1 for reliable delivery. If broker does not echo/PUBACK within the timeout, data is persisted for later transmission.

memberdef.argsstring: (MqttClient &mqttClient, const char *topicPrefix, const char *sensorType, const char *sensorId, const DateTime &now)
memberdef.name: sendPendingData

briefdescription.para: Processes and transmits pending CSV files from offline periods to the MQTT broker.

detaileddescription.para: This function scans the SD card for CSV files containing unsent sensor data from previous offline periods. Each file is converted to a JSON payload and published to the MQTT topic <topic>/recovered with QoS 1. After publishing, it waits briefly for a PUBACK handshake from the broker to confirm delivery. If the PUBACK is not received within the timeout period, the file is saved for later transmission. Files are only deleted if the publish operation succeeds. Files older than 24 hours or with invalid data are skipped.

parameterdescription.para: Reference to the MQTT client instance

simplesect.para: true if all valid files were published and deleted, false if any files remain or errors occurred
Uses QoS 1 for reliable delivery. Skips files older than 24 hours or with invalid content. Aborts if recovery exceeds time limit.
